cards.grammarNote: 안 = not (by choice). Place 안 directly before the verb: 안 먹어요 = I don't eat (I'm choosing not to). For 하다 verbs: place 안 before 하다: 공부 안 해요.
못 = cannot (due to inability or circumstances). 못 먹어요 = I can't eat. For 하다 verbs: 못 하다 → 요리 못 해요 = I can't cook.
안 + 먹어요 = choose not to eat. 고기 = meat. Placing 안 before the conjugated verb in a full sentence.
못 + 먹어요 = unable to eat. 매운 거 = spicy things (매운 = spicy, modifying 거 = thing).
술 = alcohol. 안 마셔요 = don't drink (by choice). 마시다 (to drink) → 마셔요.

cards.front: I can't drink coffee (for health reasons).
cards.grammarNote: 못 마셔요 = unable to drink. This implies a reason beyond preference — allergy, medication, etc.
안 좋아요 = not good / I don't like this. 이거 = this (informal for 이것). Can also indicate poor quality.
못 + verb modifier form: 못 먹는 = cannot eat (modifying a noun). 거 = thing. 못 먹는 음식 = food I cannot eat.
있어요 used for possession/conditions: 알레르기 있어요 = I have an allergy. 없어요 = I don't have an allergy.
안 되다 → 안 돼요 = it's not allowed / won't work / you can't. Very common for rules and prohibitions. 돼요 = it's okay / it works.
하다 verb negation with 못: place 못 before 하다. 요리 못 해요 = I can't cook. 요리 안 해요 = I don't cook (by choice).
싶지 않다 = don't want to. This is the negative of -고 싶다 (want to). More emphatic than just 안 먹어요.
